verses.text: In the morning they bind Him and deliver Him to Pilate, accusing Him of many things. Handed over. The council passes Him to Rome for death.
In the morning they bind Him and deliver Him to Pilate, accusing Him of many things. Handed over. The council passes Him to Rome for death.
In the morning they bind Him and deliver Him to Pilate, accusing Him of many things. Handed over. The council passes Him to Rome for death.
Pilate marvels that He answers nothing. Silent Lamb. He makes no defence against false charges.
Pilate marvels that He answers nothing. Silent Lamb. He makes no defence against false charges.
At the feast Pilate would release a prisoner by custom. A choice offered. The crowd must decide whom to free.
At the feast Pilate would release a prisoner by custom. A choice offered. The crowd must decide whom to free.
At the feast Pilate would release a prisoner by custom. A choice offered. The crowd must decide whom to free.
Will ye the King of the Jews? but the priests move them to ask Barabbas. Stirred to evil. The leaders sway the crowd against Christ.
Will ye the King of the Jews? but the priests move them to ask Barabbas. Stirred to evil. The leaders sway the crowd against Christ.
Will ye the King of the Jews? but the priests move them to ask Barabbas. Stirred to evil. The leaders sway the crowd against Christ.
What then with Jesus? 'Crucify him'; what evil hath he done? they cry the more. Mob fury. They demand death without a charge.
What then with Jesus? 'Crucify him'; what evil hath he done? they cry the more. Mob fury. They demand death without a charge.
What then with Jesus? 'Crucify him'; what evil hath he done? they cry the more. Mob fury. They demand death without a charge.
Pilate, to content the people, frees Barabbas and gives Jesus to be crucified. Man-pleasing. He sacrifices justice to keep the crowd quiet.
The soldiers robe Him in purple, crown Him with thorns, mock, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
The soldiers robe Him in purple, crown Him with thorns, mock, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
The soldiers robe Him in purple, crown Him with thorns, mock, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
The soldiers robe Him in purple, crown Him with thorns, mock, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
The soldiers robe Him in purple, crown Him with thorns, mock, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and lead Him out. Cruel scorn. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
Simon is made to bear His cross; they offer wine mixed with myrrh, but He takes it not. The bitter way. He will meet His suffering with a clear mind.
Simon is made to bear His cross; they offer wine mixed with myrrh, but He takes it not. The bitter way. He will meet His suffering with a clear mind.
Simon is made to bear His cross; they offer wine mixed with myrrh, but He takes it not. The bitter way. He will meet His suffering with a clear mind.
They crucify Him, part His garments, and write over Him, 'THE KING OF THE JEWS.' The cross. The King reigns from a tree.
They crucify Him, part His garments, and write over Him, 'THE KING OF THE JEWS.' The cross. The King reigns from a tree.
They crucify Him, part His garments, and write over Him, 'THE KING OF THE JEWS.' The cross. The King reigns from a tree.
Two thieves with Him; passers-by revile, 'Save thyself, come down.' Taunting unbelief. They scorn the love that holds Him there.
Two thieves with Him; passers-by revile, 'Save thyself, come down.' Taunting unbelief. They scorn the love that holds Him there.
Two thieves with Him; passers-by revile, 'Save thyself, come down.' Taunting unbelief. They scorn the love that holds Him there.
Two thieves with Him; passers-by revile, 'Save thyself, come down.' Taunting unbelief. They scorn the love that holds Him there.
The priests mock, 'He saved others; himself he cannot save.' Misread weakness. His refusal to come down was His power to save.
The priests mock, 'He saved others; himself he cannot save.' Misread weakness. His refusal to come down was His power to save.
Darkness covers the land; He cries, 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?' The dread cry. He bears the forsaking our sin deserved.
Darkness covers the land; He cries, 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?' The dread cry. He bears the forsaking our sin deserved.
Some think He calls Elias; He cries with a loud voice and gives up the ghost. Willing death. He lays down His life of His own accord.
Some think He calls Elias; He cries with a loud voice and gives up the ghost. Willing death. He lays down His life of His own accord.
Some think He calls Elias; He cries with a loud voice and gives up the ghost. Willing death. He lays down His life of His own accord.
The veil is rent; the centurion says, 'Truly this man was the Son of God.' Opened way and witness. His death tears the veil and wins a confession.
The veil is rent; the centurion says, 'Truly this man was the Son of God.' Opened way and witness. His death tears the veil and wins a confession.
Women behold from afar, who followed and ministered to Him. Faithful watchers. When others flee, the women remain near.
Women behold from afar, who followed and ministered to Him. Faithful watchers. When others flee, the women remain near.
Joseph of Arimathaea boldly begs the body; Pilate marvels He is already dead. Bold disciple. A secret follower steps forward at the cross.
Joseph of Arimathaea boldly begs the body; Pilate marvels He is already dead. Bold disciple. A secret follower steps forward at the cross.
Joseph of Arimathaea boldly begs the body; Pilate marvels He is already dead. Bold disciple. A secret follower steps forward at the cross.
He wraps Him in linen, lays Him in a tomb, and rolls a stone; the women watch. Honoured burial. Christ is given an honourable grave.
He wraps Him in linen, lays Him in a tomb, and rolls a stone; the women watch. Honoured burial. Christ is given an honourable grave.
He wraps Him in linen, lays Him in a tomb, and rolls a stone; the women watch. Honoured burial. Christ is given an honourable grave.
